summary: This cluster of research focuses on the taxonomic revision of the diatom genus *Amphora sensu lato*, specifically through the integration of molecular phylogenetics and morphological analysis. Studies demonstrated that the subgenus *Halamphora* is phylogenetically distinct, supporting its reclassification as a separate genus, and led to the formal description of a new species, *Amphora terroris*. The work underscores the critical role of morphological data in resolving taxonomic ambiguities within this globally distributed group, which inhabits diverse marine and estuarine environments.
